arkhe 0.3.0.9000
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Data Replacement

Source: R/AllGenerics.R, R/clean.R
replace.Rd

Removes empty row/column or row/column with missing values or zeros.

replace_NA(x, ...)

# S4 method for matrix
replace_NA(x, value = 0)

Arguments

x

A matrix, a data.frame or a *Matrix object.
...

Currently not used.
value

A possible value to replace missing values of x.

See also

Other utilities: remove()

Author

N. Frerebeau

Examples

## Create a count data matrix
X <- CountMatrix(sample(1:10, 25, TRUE), nrow = 5, ncol = 5)
k <- sample(1:25, 3, FALSE)

## Add zeros
X[k] <- 0L
## Remove row with zeros
remove_zero(X, margin = 1)

#> 3 rows were removed:
#> * row1 (n=1)
#> * row2 (n=1)
#> * row4 (n=1)
#> <CountMatrix: 2 x 5>
#>      col1 col2 col3 col4 col5
#> row3   10    6    7    8    8
#> row5    5    7    6    3    5
## Remove column with zeros
remove_zero(X, margin = 2)

#> 3 columns were removed:
#> * col1 (n=1)
#> * col3 (n=1)
#> * col4 (n=1)
#> <CountMatrix: 5 x 2>
#>      col2 col5
#> row1    1    3
#> row2    2    9
#> row3    6    8
#> row4    1    2
#> row5    7    5

## Add NA
X[k] <- NA
## Remove row with zeros
remove_NA(X, margin = 1)

#> 3 rows were removed:
#> * row1 (n=1)
#> * row2 (n=1)
#> * row4 (n=1)
#> <CountMatrix: 2 x 5>
#>      col1 col2 col3 col4 col5
#> row3   10    6    7    8    8
#> row5    5    7    6    3    5
## Remove column with zeros
remove_NA(X, margin = 2)

#> 3 columns were removed:
#> * col1 (n=1)
#> * col3 (n=1)
#> * col4 (n=1)
#> <CountMatrix: 5 x 2>
#>      col2 col5
#> row1    1    3
#> row2    2    9
#> row3    6    8
#> row4    1    2
#> row5    7    5

Contents
